title: Remembering Actor Patrick Muldoon: A Career in Entertainment
summary: Patrick Muldoon, an American actor and producer, passed away at 57 due to a heart attack at his home. He gained recognition for his role as Austin Reed on the NBC soap opera Days of Our Lives from 1992 to 1995, with a brief return in 2011. He also starred in the 1997 sci-fi film Starship Troopers as Zander Barcalow and appeared in Melrose Place as Richard Hart. Co-stars like Alison Sweeney and Dina Meyer paid tribute, highlighting his kindness and humor. Born in California, he studied at the University of Southern California and played football there. After graduating, he started with Saved By the Bell and later produced films such as The Tribes of Palos Verdes. He was involved in music with his band The Sleeping Masses and had upcoming projects like Dirty Hands. He is survived by his partner and family, leaving a legacy in the entertainment industry.

background_read: Patrick Muldoon was an actor born in San Pedro, California, who rose to fame in the 1990s through roles in popular TV shows and films. He balanced acting with producing and music, showcasing versatility in the entertainment industry. His death has prompted reflections on his contributions and personal qualities, as reported by media sources like the BBC.
Article_Structure: This article reports on the death of actor Patrick Muldoon at age 57 from a heart attack. Main Points include his notable roles in Days of Our Lives and Starship Troopers, tributes from co-stars, and his work as a producer. Purpose is to inform the public about his passing and celebrate his career. Evidence Evaluation relies on manager confirmation and co-star statements, which are credible but emotional. Author Credibility is based on BBC reporting, known for factual accuracy. Methodology involves citing media reports and direct quotes, providing a straightforward news account.

perspectives.description: Muldoon's roles in TV and film left a mark on entertainment, remembered by fans and colleagues.
